data.title: Eric Balchunas Says HedgeEye Launches Democratic and Republican Prediction ETFs
data.description: Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas said on X that HedgeEye has joined the prediction market ETF race by launching Democratic and Republican prediction ETFs. According to Odaily, each ETF will track the election performance of its party in U.S. federal elections.

data.title: U.S. Finalizes Up To 12.5% Tariffs On South Korea, Japan Over Forced Labor Concerns
data.description:
According to Yonhap, the Trump administration said on Thursday it will impose tariffs of as much as 12.5% on South Korea, Japan and other trading partners over forced labor concerns. The Office of the U.S. Trade Representative unveiled the new duties, which range from 10% to 12.5% and cover dozens of countries after Section 301 investigations into 60 economies found they had not taken sufficient steps to ban imports made with forced labor.

The announcement came as the administration's temporary 10% global tariffs were due to expire on Friday, after the Supreme Court struck down the Trump administration's country-specific "reciprocal tariffs" in February. Last month, the USTR had proposed a 12.5% tariff on South Korea and other economies over the same issue. The South Korean government urged Washington to reconsider the proposal, calling it unwarranted and disproportionate given the country's circumstances. Separately, the USTR has also been probing South Korea, China, Japan and 13 other trading partners for alleged unfair trade practices tied to structural excess capacity and production.

data.title: Nvidia Signs $1.5 Billion Packaging Accord With Amkor
data.description:
Nvidia Corp. signed a $1.5 billion agreement with Amkor Technology Inc. to help bolster the chip packaging company’s facilities, according to Bloomberg.

The deal is part of a broader push to expand semiconductor operations in the US.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Overseas Buyers Chase US Crude as Wars Escalate
data.description: Asian and European buyers are stepping up demand for US crude, among the first signs that traders are growing more concerned about adequate oil supplies as geopolitical flashpoints multiply, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Trump Threatens Strikes On Iranian Bridges And Power Plants Over Strait Of Hormuz Attacks
data.description: According to Axios, President Donald Trump said the United States would bomb and destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant, including sites near Tehran, each time Iran attacks a ship in the Strait of Hormuz. Trump said the threat comes as he considers both a ceasefire and a larger military escalation, and he said a ceasefire would require Iran to stop attacking ships and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said Iran has attacked more than 30 commercial vessels over the past three months, including several attacks after the U.S. and Iran signed a June memorandum of understanding calling for a ceasefire. Regional sources said Qatari mediators are still trying to reach a deal to stop the fighting and reopen the strait. Iran’s Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said the Strait of Hormuz will not return to its pre-war status, Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned of a powerful and decisive response, and Brig. Gen. Seyed Majid Mousavi threatened power outages in other countries in the region if the U.S. bombs bridges and power plants in Iran.

data.title: US Cyber Test Finds Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 Trails Top American Models
data.description:
The US Commerce Department said Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 scored well below leading US frontier models in a cyber-focused test run by CAISI with British experts. The assessment found Kimi K3 lagged on ExploitBench and The Last Ones, though the report said the gap may be overstated by test limits, according to BeInCrypto, including the use of safety-off settings for US models and only partial testing of Kimi K3.

Moonshot launched Kimi K3 on July 16 and plans a full release on July 27. The report came a day after Washington accused Moonshot of building the model with stolen US technology.

data.title: Musk Says It Is Too Late To Stop AI, Sees Superintelligence In Five Years
data.description: Elon Musk said it is too late to stop AI and that even he could not halt it, adding that AI may exceed the sum of human intelligence in about five years. According to BeInCrypto, he told The Economist that there seems to be “inexorable progress” in AI and robots, while still warning the risk is not zero. Musk also said humans may no longer be in charge within 10 years.

data.title: Experts Warn Bitcoin Quantum Risk Is Being Underestimated
data.description:
Stefano Gogioso and Daniela Herrmann warned that the crypto industry is treating quantum computing risk too casually, arguing the focus should be on the odds and cost of a Bitcoin break rather than a fixed “Q-Day” date. According to BeInCrypto, Gogioso said even a 2% chance of failure by 2030 could justify action, while Herrmann said estimates for practical quantum computing have shortened rapidly.

The panel cited Google research suggesting Bitcoin’s elliptic-curve cryptography could require fewer than 500,000 physical qubits to break, and said migration to quantum-resistant cryptography could take years.

data.title: SK Hynix Billionaire Chey Tae-won Set for New Divorce Ruling
data.description: SK Hynix Inc. billionaire Chey Tae-won is set to receive a fresh court ruling in his long-running divorce dispute with his former wife, bringing the acrimonious case closer to a conclusion, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Pentagon awards Oracle nearly $7 billion contract over 10 years
data.description:
According to CNBC, the Pentagon on Thursday announced a contract with Oracle worth almost $7 billion over a decade, and Oracle shares rose about 3% in extended trading. The deal covers Oracle software used in on-premises data centers for military branches, the U.S. intelligence community and the Coast Guard, while the Central Intelligence Agency was Oracle's first customer.

Kirsten Davies, the Department of Defense's chief information officer, said the agency is saving at least $441 million for taxpayers by changing how it buys Oracle on-premises capabilities. Oracle shares remain down 38% this year as investors worry that AI could hurt growth at software incumbents, and the company is also taking on tens of billions of dollars in debt to build AI data centers.

data.title: Trump administration to impose new tariffs on 60 trade partners
data.description: According to CNBC, the Trump administration will impose new tariffs just after midnight ET Friday on 60 trade partners over alleged forced-labor violations, with duties set between 10% and 12.5% covering more than 99% of U.S. trade. The new levies will replace President Donald Trump's temporary 10% global tariffs when those duties expire, and they will not stack on top of existing Section 232 steel and aluminum tariffs. The Office of the U.S. Trade Representative said it could not estimate how much revenue the tariffs will generate. U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said in Senate testimony Wednesday that the administration will continue using tariffs to negotiate deals, support reindustrialization, protect American workers, raise wages and reduce the trade deficit.

data.title: Goldman, JPMorgan Launch $250 Million AI Junk Bond Trading Products
data.description:
Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. launched products this week that let investors quickly reduce or increase exposure to tech industry debt, according to Bloomberg.

The moves come as concerns build over hyperscalers' large future bond sales to fund artificial intelligence investments, with the products sized at $250 million at a time.

data.title: AI Debt Fears Spark Tech Bond Selloff
data.description:
Bonds of some of the biggest US technology companies fell Thursday as investors renewed worries about the scale of debt financing behind the artificial-intelligence boom, according to Bloomberg.

The selloff came as conflict in the Middle East escalated, adding to pressure on the sector's debt.

data.title: Japan Becomes a Net Petrochemical Importer as Naphtha Prices Surge
data.description:
Japan's petrochemical trade pattern has reversed, with imports exceeding exports. According to Jin10, Nikkei reported that the Iran war disrupted supplies of naphtha derived from crude oil, boosting Japan's imports of basic petrochemical feedstocks used to make plastics and other products, and making purchases from China and South Korea more cost-effective.

Trade data showed that from March to May this year, Japan's ethylene imports rose 14-fold year on year. Imports of butadiene, a raw material for synthetic rubber, more than doubled, while benzene imports rose more than 28-fold. At the same time, exports of these products declined, with ethylene exports in May falling to nearly zero.

According to the Japan Petrochemical Industry Association, Japan's ethylene imports exceeded exports by 19,000 metric tons in April and by 15,000 metric tons in May. It was the first monthly instance of ethylene imports exceeding exports since comparable data became available in 2000.

data.title: Bitcoin News Says BIP-110 Forced Signaling To Begin Around August 8
data.description: Bitcoin News posted on X that BIP-110 forced signaling will begin around August 8. According to Odaily, a dashboard can be used to track differences between the legacy chain and the BIP-110 chain.

data.title: U.S. Foreign Central Bank Holdings of Treasurys at $24.814 Billion for Week Ended July 17
data.description: According to Jin10, foreign central banks held $24.814 billion in U.S. Treasurys for the week ended July 17, compared with $639 million previously.

data.title: US Treasury Calls Out China for Currency Transparency
data.description: The US Treasury Department said China has a "relative lack of transparency" in how it manages its currency, according to Bloomberg. The criticism came in a semiannual foreign-exchange report released Thursday and singled China out among US trading partners.

data.title: Wellington Shuns Data-Center Debt Deals on Property Value Concerns
data.description:
Wellington Management is passing on data-center debt deals as risks rise, property values remain uncertain and returns fall, according to Bloomberg.

The firm’s stance underscores growing caution around financing for data centers amid concern over how much the properties are worth.

data.title: Hyperliquid Slips Below $60 As Multicoin, Paradigm Unstake $291M In HYPE
data.description: Hyperliquid’s HYPE fell below $60 after two major funds unstaked about $291 million of the token in days, stoking fears of selling. Multicoin said it is not selling and on-chain data showed the tokens moving to custody rather than to market, according to BeInCrypto, while Paradigm has not commented. HYPE was last trading at $58, about 24% below its June record of $76.70.

data.title: Clarity Act expected to miss its window before Congress' summer break
data.description: The crypto market structure bill known as the Clarity Act is likely to miss the deadline to clear the Senate before lawmakers leave for their summer break, Majority Leader Thune said. The bill may still get a start before then, according to CoinDesk, but leadership signaled the full effort will not be completed in time.
